jokes.text: What is this, jeff? Whose panties are these? What the fuck is this? It's your car. What are these doing in there? All right, pull the fuck over.
jokes.setup: Susie finds women's panties in Jeff's glove compartment
jokes.punchline: The escalating rage and accusation creates immediate relationship crisis
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb setup that launches the episode's main crisis. Susie's explosive reaction is perfectly in character and the situation is both relatable and mortifying.

jokes.text: But I left my pants in the dressing room. Jesus.
jokes.setup: Fire alarm goes off during pants fitting
jokes.punchline: Larry's concern about his pants during an emergency evacuation
jokes.explanation: Larry's misplaced priorities during an emergency are classic character comedy, though not exceptional.

jokes.text: You're officer krupke. Familiar with 'west side story'?
jokes.setup: Larry meets a real police officer named Krupke
jokes.punchline: The coincidental connection to the musical character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup that Larry would absolutely fixate on this coincidence. The officer's complete ignorance makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: By the way, they wanted to say 'fuck you.' But in the '50s on broadway, sondheim, he couldn't write 'fuck you.' So 'krup you' is a substitute for 'fuck you.'
jokes.setup: Larry explaining the musical reference to the clueless officer
jokes.punchline: The pedantic Broadway trivia lesson about profanity substitution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David moment - turning a simple interaction into an unwanted history lesson. The profanity explanation to a cop is deliciously awkward.

jokes.text: Some guy told me to go fuck my face once. He went to jail.
jokes.setup: Discussion of creative profanity substitutes
jokes.punchline: Officer's matter-of-fact delivery of bizarre anecdote
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered non-sequitur that's both shocking and hilarious. The deadpan delivery makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Larry singing the entire West Side Story song to the confused officer
jokes.setup: Officer doesn't know the song
jokes.punchline: Larry's compulsive need to perform the entire thing
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry behavior - can't let something go. The extended singing creates awkward comedy through Larry's obliviousness.

jokes.text: I told her they were your panties. What do you mean they're my panties? No no, I told her that they're your panties, that you like wearing women's panties.
jokes.setup: Jeff needs Larry's help with the panties situation
jokes.punchline: The reveal that Jeff blamed Larry for the panties by claiming he's a cross-dresser
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the central premise. Jeff's desperation leading to this absurd lie is perfectly in character and creates the episode's main comedic engine.

jokes.text: Odd yet brilliant. Completely and utterly brilliant. My best one ever maybe.
jokes.setup: Jeff convincing Larry to go along with the panties lie
jokes.punchline: Jeff's genuine pride in his terrible plan
jokes.explanation: Jeff's delusional self-congratulation is funny but not exceptional. Good character work though.

jokes.text: Larry, you have a security tag on your pants.
jokes.setup: Larry wearing the stolen pants to a social gathering
jokes.punchline: The obvious security tag drawing attention
jokes.explanation: Good callback that keeps the pants subplot alive. The visual absurdity works well.

jokes.text: I really have a hard time with 'how we met' stories, okay? So I'm gonna... Well, I wanna hear it. We don't have to tell it. No, you can hear it. I find it cloying and annoying.
jokes.setup: Couple wants to tell their meet-cute story
jokes.punchline: Larry's brutal honesty about hating these stories
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David social awkwardness. His inability to fake interest in social niceties is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? Oh my god. This is awful. I'm not even joking. Who made that? Us. It's beautiful. You made it? Give me my money back.
jokes.setup: Larry tries lemonade from kids' stand
jokes.punchline: His brutal honest review and demand for refund from children
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry taking things too far with children. The escalation from bad lemonade to full confrontation is perfectly cringe.

jokes.text: Bald asshole!
jokes.setup: Larry's confrontation with the lemonade kids
jokes.punchline: Child's surprisingly harsh insult
jokes.explanation: The shock of a child using such language is funny, though somewhat cheap. Good escalation of the conflict.

jokes.text: What are you, self-conscious? No, you're looking me over. I was not looking you over, paranoid.
jokes.setup: Jeff is acting strange because of the panties lie
jokes.punchline: Larry noticing Jeff's weird behavior and Jeff's defensive reaction
jokes.explanation: Good development of the panties subplot. Jeff's paranoia about how to act around Larry is believable and funny.

jokes.text: I think they deserve an adult critique. If they're gonna charge a dollar and give you this much lemonade... I felt a little ripped off.
jokes.setup: Mother confronts Larry about criticizing her kids' lemonade
jokes.punchline: Larry's serious justification for harsh critique of children's business
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - treating children's lemonade stand like a real business. His complete lack of awareness about appropriate behavior with kids is hilarious.

jokes.text: They're in that little kid bubble. They're buds, and you just... You know, just piss all over it. I didn't piss on the buds. No, I helped the buds. I'm helping the buds grow. I'm good for buds.
jokes.setup: Mother using 'buds' metaphor for innocent children
jokes.punchline: Larry turning it into literal discussion about plant buds
jokes.explanation: Larry's literal interpretation of the metaphor is classic character work. The 'I'm good for buds' line is memorable.

jokes.text: Why would you have to audition? It's based on you.
jokes.setup: Cheryl auditioning for part based on her own life experience
jokes.punchline: Virginia pointing out the absurdity of auditioning to play yourself
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on the absurdity of the situation. Good observational humor about the entertainment industry.

jokes.text: Why hold a hat when you could just put it on your head? Wear a hat. Yeah, exactly. But it's not a hat, it's a pair of pants with a security...
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his logic for wearing stolen pants
jokes.punchline: Jerry's comparison to wearing a hat vs carrying it, then the reality check
jokes.explanation: Good callback development with Jerry enabling Larry's logic before reality hits. The hat analogy is clever.

jokes.text: Cheryl's entire audition performance as George's panicked ex-wife on the plane
jokes.setup: Audition scene recreating airplane emergency call
jokes.punchline: The TiVo guy priority over life-or-death conversation
jokes.explanation: Exceptional scene that perfectly captures the absurd priorities of modern life. Cheryl's performance is brilliant and the escalation from life-threatening emergency to TiVo concerns is perfectly structured.

jokes.text: I just want you to know... It's just a TiVo guy.
jokes.setup: Wife trying to express final words during plane emergency
jokes.punchline: Husband dismissing her crisis for appliance repair
jokes.explanation: Peak Seinfeld-style comedy. The casual dismissal of a life-threatening situation for mundane concerns is perfectly executed and highly quotable.

jokes.text: That was insane, okay? I mean, that was... That was perfect. Perfect. Everything we want.
jokes.setup: Cheryl's excellent audition performance
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-the-top praise that reveals his bias
jokes.explanation: Larry's obvious bias toward his ex-wife is believable character work, though not exceptionally funny on its own.

jokes.text: Well, a girl like that, if you can get her, it's like 'let's get on board the virginia sloan, toot toot. Next stop, comedy town.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about casting Virginia over Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Jerry's ridiculous train metaphor for Virginia's talent
jokes.explanation: Jerry's elaborate metaphor is charmingly overblown and quotable, though somewhat forced.

jokes.text: There's a difference between lost and gone? Yes. Lost is different than gone. They're lost and gone. If they're lost, they'd be here in the lost and found. They're gone and that's the difference.
jokes.setup: Store clerk explaining missing pants
jokes.punchline: Absurd semantic argument about lost vs. gone
jokes.explanation: Good Curb-style semantic argument, though the distinction is somewhat forced. The circular logic is funny.

jokes.text: Good for you. I got a sign at my house, okay? It says 'if somebody takes your pants, you take theirs.' That's my sign.
jokes.setup: Store policy about not being responsible for lost items
jokes.punchline: Larry's made-up retaliatory sign
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic creating a fake policy to justify his behavior. The specificity and absurdity make it highly quotable.

jokes.text: Call me hester.
jokes.setup: Clerk calls the security tag a scarlet letter
jokes.punchline: Larry's literary reference to The Scarlet Letter protagonist
jokes.explanation: Perfect literary callback that shows Larry's education while being economical and witty. The reference works on multiple levels.

jokes.text: Oh, you know, virginia went to the bathroom and he asked me if I was interested in a menage a trois.
jokes.setup: Cheryl describing lunch with Virginia and Dennis
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of threesome proposition
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Larry's reaction and introduces new plot element. The casual way Cheryl mentions it adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: If you're gonna have a menage a trois with anybody, it's gonna be with me, not with him. Why would I want to be in a menage a trois with you?
jokes.setup: Larry's jealous reaction to threesome proposition
jokes.punchline: His assumption he'd be included and Cheryl's immediate rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry jealousy and possessiveness over his ex-wife, followed by immediate deflation. Great character work.

jokes.text: Curious? Did you say curious?
jokes.setup: Dennis suggesting Cheryl might be interested in threesome
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked reaction to the implication
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that shows Larry processing uncomfortable information about his ex-wife.

jokes.text: Just because cheryl is curious doesn't mean you have any right to get mad at me, okay? Curious? Did you say curious?
jokes.setup: Dennis defending his proposition
jokes.punchline: Larry's shock at learning Cheryl might be interested
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing new information. Larry's repeated 'curious?' shows his processing shock.

jokes.text: I have a little problem with my underwear. All of a sudden out of nowhere? What, do you have a new brand or something? Yeah, I do.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to act naturally about the fake panties
jokes.punchline: His obvious discomfort creating suspicion
jokes.explanation: Larry's terrible acting creating more problems. The callback to the panties situation builds tension.

jokes.text: I'm trying to play like slight transvestite. Slight transvestite? Yes. It's a comfort thing. Don't add a transvestite level to this.
jokes.setup: Larry struggling with how to act wearing women's underwear
jokes.punchline: Jeff's horror at Larry's interpretation of the role
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the panties subplot with Larry overthinking his performance. The 'slight transvestite' concept is absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: I got a call from virginia. She can't do the part. She's in a neck brace. Oh my god.
jokes.setup: Virginia's sudden unavailability
jokes.punchline: The mysterious neck injury
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the episode's conclusion. The mysterious injury creates intrigue.

jokes.text: There's only two ways you can injure your neck. One is a car accident. The other's cunnilingus.
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out how Virginia injured her neck
jokes.punchline: Larry's binary logic about neck injury causes
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pseudo-scientific observation that's both crude and logical in its own twisted way. The binary thinking is perfectly in character and highly quotable.

jokes.text: Nothing. Cheryl. Cunnilingus.
jokes.setup: Finding no car damage
jokes.punchline: Larry's matter-of-fact conclusion about the threesome
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of Larry's logical deduction. The economy of the exchange makes it memorable.

jokes.text: And I submit she performed it on you in a threesome.
jokes.setup: Larry confronting Cheryl about Virginia's neck injury
jokes.punchline: His formal 'prosecution' language for crude accusation
jokes.explanation: Larry's legalistic language applied to sexual situations is peak character comedy. The formality makes the crude content even funnier.

jokes.text: You know, I never saw michelangelo in a brace.
jokes.setup: Cheryl suggesting painting can cause neck injury
jokes.punchline: Larry's art history reference to disprove her point
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical reference that's both erudite and absurd. Larry's pedantic tendency to use examples from high culture for low arguments is perfect.

jokes.text: ♪ gee, officer krupke, krup you. ♪
jokes.setup: Larry walking past the lemonade stand again
jokes.punchline: Singing the West Side Story song with the profanity substitute
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the opening Officer Krupke interaction. The song coming back at this moment provides good continuity.

jokes.text: She was driving dennis's car, asshole know-it-all. His car is all fucked up.
jokes.setup: Larry's confident cunnilingus theory
jokes.punchline: The theory completely debunked by simple facts
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of Larry's elaborate theory. Jeff calling him 'asshole know-it-all' while proving him wrong is satisfying.

jokes.text: She was driving dennis's car, asshole know-it-all. His car's... His car is all fucked up.
jokes.setup: Larry's certainty about no car accident
jokes.punchline: Jeff revealing Larry's detective work was flawed
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the investigation subplot, showing Larry's overconfidence leading to wrong conclusion.

jokes.text: Okay, fine. You don't have to do this here. I don't care. Okay, here. Oh my god! Jesus christ! Who are you? I'm larry david. I happen to enjoy wearing women's panties.
jokes.setup: Officer demanding Larry remove the stolen pants
jokes.punchline: Larry's public declaration about wearing women's underwear
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the episode's main plot thread. Larry's matter-of-fact delivery of the absurd statement in front of everyone is comedy gold. This is the episode's peak moment.

jokes.text: Larry forced to remove the stolen pants in front of everyone, revealing women's panties
jokes.setup: Multiple plot threads converging
jokes.punchline: Ultimate public humiliation combining all storylines
jokes.explanation: Perfect convergence of all episode storylines. The visual payoff ties together panties, stolen pants, and public humiliation brilliantly.
